reasoning: Bobby Finke's 1500m long course world record was set in August 2024 at the Paris Olympics and was still standing as of November 2025. Historically, the men's 1500m freestyle record is incredibly resilient, often standing for about a decade (Sun Yang held it for 12 years; Hackett for 10). The period from November through February is predominantly the short course (25m pool) season globally. During this time, there are virtually no major international long course (50m pool) competitions that elite distance swimmers peak for. Therefore, the chance of a competitor breaking this gruelling 1500m LC record in an off-season or early-season meet before February 2026 is vanishingly small. The only other risk would be a delayed doping disqualification from Paris 2024, but after over a year this is also highly improbable. To satisfy the requirement for uncertainty increasing over time and remaining within the 0.05–0.95 bounds, I predict 0.95, 0.94, and 0.92.
